reviews.review_msg: The class is all about blood. It has potential to be interesting but ruined by the prof. His voice is beyond irritating and he makes you do so many useless activities. There is no lab component to the course yet he plans "lab visits" every other lecture and makes you write a lab report with no explanation. He's a geek don't waste your time or money

The class is all about blood. It has potential to be interesting but ruined by the prof. His voice is beyond irritating and he makes you do so many useless activities. There is no lab component to the course yet he plans "lab visits" every other lecture and makes you write a lab report with no explanation. He's a geek don't waste your time or money
Caring and nice prof. However the TAs ruined this course with their grading. It's actually unbelievable how harsh they marked assignments, totally ruining Angel's teaching efforts.

Angel is one of the best profs I've ever had. Not only is she passionate about what she teaches, but she also clearly puts in the effort to engage her students in the material as well. The most accessible prof and super accommodating too, if you ever have any issues with assignments, just reach out to her! ANGEL SO AWESOME

If you've never taken a class with Angel Kennedy,  what are you doing !!! Truly the most passionate, engaged professor I've ever had. When she says she cares about students, she really means it. Her lectures are clearly so meaningfully planned and well-thought-out.  Expect to do the work because the projects are profound and thought invoking.

Angel is one of the most kindest profs within the faculty. In all of my four years I have never met a prof that cares so deeply for the learning of her students. She is always flexible and frequently checks in with students about their mental health and clearing up any concepts that may be unclear. Cannot recommend her enough for future students!

Angel is such a sweet prof. She's really passionate about planetary health and I can tell that she is growing in her passion as a prof. She always considers student feedback, is easily accessible outside of class, and even holds little coffee chats as her office hours just so that she can get to know students. I love u Angel!!!!

Angel is quite literally the sweetest prof I've ever encountered. Her teaching style is flexible, adaptable, and accessible. She truly cares about the well-being in students, which is reflected by her doing check ins, extending deadlines, and more. Take her if you can!!!
marks really hard. his lectures are super dry and boring. I learnt nothing in this class

HSCI 333 (formerly 372) was my third course with Dr. L, and he didn't disappoint. Dr. L is incredibly intelligent and really makes you think about science concepts from a more holistic POV. I can't say enough great things about him. Show that you care and that you put in the effort, and your grade will reflect that. Favourite HSCI prof.

I loveee Mark! I had him for Hsci 100 in my first year as well. He is a great prof ! I would definitely take another class with him! He is an entertaining prof, sometimes he will pull out a game that is related to the course material and entertain his students.

Mark is a great prof! This course (which is now HSCI 333) was very interesting and I actually learned a lot. If you are a science student this course won't be too hard. Few quizzes throughout the semester which you can work on with others and assignments weren't too difficult. The marking is fair. I would definitely take more classes with Mark!
Sweetest and the most humble professor ever.

Dr.Lechner is the absolute best prof at SFU. Although the material covered in his class was not something everyone would find interesting, he made the experience very enjoyable. He is very kind, and genuinely just a great person. So take ANY course with him. ;)mark rocks my world

An amazingly kind person. . .one of the nicest people I've met. Class was relatively easy and the final was very straight forward. However,he has a strict marking curve so you probably need 97+ to get an A+. I would recommend this class in a heart beat though.

Dr. Lechner is a really great prof. His notes are very straightforward, and he explains everything very clearly. He always tries to find a way to give you bonus marks at any given occasion. The course is a bit heavy, but the assignments are for the most part enjoyable. Very approachable; I would definitely take a class with him again!
